Hey guys, welcome back to module one of 10x launches. In this lesson, we are actually going to be going deeper into the coaching the conversion method and I really want to just start off with a brief two minute history of how this method came to be. It was really a unique clash of circumstances that led to the insights into breakthroughs and really the implementation or necessity of writing copy, especially launch copy, using this method. So just to give you a little bit of context, it was really about two years ago when I was hitting my stride as an in-demand launch copywriter, you know, writing or consulting for some of my own personal heroes and mentors and executing those massive six and seven figure how the heck did they even pull those off type of launches. So that's really the time period that I started, you know, needing to develop this as great as that was that period of my career. The problem was I was also really hitting my stride as a Hollywood screenwriter. It was totally by fluke an accident that projects that I had been working on, you know, three or four or five years prior were finally starting to get steam. So one project got optioned by an award-winning producer and we went through the rounds and rewrites and getting directors involved and all the things that happen when you have to get something production ready. You know, in another instance, you know, right off the heels of that, I was hired to write a brand new screenplay from scratch for a production company here in Canada. So there was a lot going on. It was a hectic time and this is actually further complicated by the fact that I was also hitting my stride as a coach, what I called an expression engineer. So during the peak of all this, I was running about 15 hours worth of coaching sessions per week, really unpacking what it was. I was blocking people from living their fullest expression and helping them re-engineer their lives and realities to really kind of support that full expression. So all this kind of the common denominator was fueled by this deep, deep, deep kind of fascination with what drives human behavior and catalyzes transformation. But I was also equally inspired and influenced by marketing and persuasion legends like Eugene Schwartz, Robert Caldini, Robert Collier, as well as many personal growth legends and psychologists and transformational leaders like Steven Covey and Maxwell Martz and Victor Frankel. So all this is going on and then finally, here was kind of the clincher. I took mind blown a bit too seriously. All of these things were coming right immediately off the heels of having a stroke. You know, I took a vacation from my last full-time job and on day two woke up half paralyzed and you know, I recovered fully, thankful for that. But coming out of that and really recovering and having all these things going on, I had maybe about three or four hours of focused productive time per day to get all these things done, which on the surface made no sense and something had to be cut. But at the same time, there was this very clear message that I had to live my own full expression, that I couldn't amputate any of these interests in coaching or screenwriting or copywriting in order to meet the demands, the growing demands of each of them. I had to find a way to really embrace the slash between these rules to integrate them, to leverage them strategically and finally really to bring all things to one thing. And that's what really ignited this journey. It was to create a methodology or process that would allow me to get exceptional results for clients in less time because I didn't have all the time to do it while also embracing all these aspects of myself that were yearning for expression. And at the same time, you know, it just turned out that they all synergized much, much better than what I expected. But really what it all comes down to, there's been this question ever since I started my marketing journey over, you know, a decade ago. The big question that's always fueled me was this, why do over 97% of qualified, perfectly targeted leads not buy? And more importantly, why the heck do we settle for that? You know, as coaches, educators, entrepreneurs and program creators who genuinely care about our audience, and I mean really deeply care about them, why are we so willing to let over 97% of them go underserved? And what if instead of giving up on them, we made it not just our right, but our responsibility to give every single person who enters our funnel the best possible chance of becoming a customer. And the way I like to equate this is let's say you had 10 kids and only one or half of one was open to receiving your advice and guidance. What would you do about the other nine? Would you give up on them? Or would you do everything in your power to just really deeply understand where they're at and do what you had to do to empathize, to open them up and to create a new context for them in which they are ready and empowered to receive your guidance. So it's really the same level of care I want you to bring to your audience. And finally, this was all fueled by a very intense curiosity. You know, why is it that one-on-one or collaborative selling, often done by coaches, can convert anywhere from 20% to 50%? Literally 10x what typical online marketing funnels can generate. And of course a biggie, how can we start bridging that gap at scale? So that really is the inquiry that has fueled this method and that's where coaching the conversion comes into play. It's really picking up where traditional direct response and persuasion techniques have left us, you know, with conversions of 1% to 3% and bringing and integrating and leveraging the mindsets, tools and strategies that are used by some of the world's best sales people and coaches and therapists and leaders, people who ethically influence behavior with positive and predictable outcomes. That is what we're doing here. And now here's the main premise that is underlying this whole process. This is based on the fact that the version of someone who buys your product is fundamentally different than the person who initially came into your sequence or funnel. And it's the job of the copywriter or the conversion coach to understand that transformation, to spark or catalyze that transformation and to coach and facilitate that transformation to completion. You know, it's our job, it's within our job description to empathize and understand their current context, reflected back to them as accurately and specifically as possible which in turn earns us their trust and the authority to invite them to reshift and rethink their thoughts and attitudes and beliefs in a way that leads them to being more empowered to purchase your product. So therefore a sale is so much less about a one size fits all template or a pushy persuasion tactic and so much more about flexing some very real and genuine empathy chops and coaching your lead to the point where they're empowered enough to be a successful buyer. And when you do this, you know, in a strategic way with a clear methodology like I'm going to show you in the coming modules, your marketing truly does become a gift. It's a gift that can release your prospect from old wounds, from doubts, from fears around the topic or the thing they're trying to solve. And it truly does give them a chance to feel heard and understood more deeply than ever before. But most of all, it gives them a chance. It gives them a new sense of optimism and hope and chance to evolve in a direction that matters to them and express themselves in new meaningful ways that make your product the natural solution. And that is why marketing can be an ultimate win-win. You know, at its heart, marketing is a catalyst for change. And for anyone who ever thought copywriting was a scary esoteric thing where you had to memorize a hundred different triggers, formulas, templates, I invite you right now to just drop the term copywriting completely and instead just simply learn how to coach the conversion to give your customer the gifts of intimacy, of understanding and evolution and be that trusted authority figure in their life who can successfully catalyze and lead it. So in the rest of this module, we're going to cover the key principles that really set the foundation for you becoming a successful conversion coach. And then later in the modules, you learn very specifically how to apply those principles and those characteristics across every phase of your launch or evergreen funnel, particularly within your email sequences so that you can generate more sales while building greater trust and intimacy with your audience. So that's what we have in store. But before we move on, I want to leave you with this image. This is called Japanese joinery art. And there's a link below on the slide or in this video where you can actually see a video that went viral a little back on Facebook of this woodworking technique where these pieces just slide together. And it got shared like crazy, probably because of how satisfying it is to see two complex intricate and nuanced objects just seamlessly click into place. And I want you to imagine your customer as one of those blocks and your product as the other. And your marketing as this invisible but powerful force that effortlessly brings and binds them together. So traditional persuasion and pushy sales techniques would really try to just jam and force them together, which inevitably would break one and at the very least create intense friction. You know, so add best forcing an unsustainable long term fit. So when your marketing can act as a force that shaves both pieces, carves the beliefs and shizzles away at the resistance over the course of your funnel, you get this perfect effortless fit. So those carvings are really the beliefs you need to chisel away with over the course of your funnel. You know, your marketing, your messaging and the sequences that drive them. It's really all about just engineering engineering that context for that natural conversion to occur. So it's really where we set up the conditions and circumstances in which your offer naturally syncs up and connects to your customers needs and desires harmoniously. So with that in mind, I'm going to meet you in lesson two, where we'll go a bit deeper into the process of coaching the conversion and a few other of the key concepts that really set the foundation. I'll see you there.
